fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Michigan Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Michigan - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity michigan, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to michigan, speech-language pathologist compact michigan, michigan speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Speech-Language Pathology
board.parent_dept: Michigan Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs (LARA)

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Michigan.

license_types.title: Temporary SLP License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Earn a Master's Degree in SLP
steps.description: Complete a master's degree from a CAA-accredited program.

steps.title: Pass the Praxis Examination
steps.description: Achieve a passing score on Praxis (test code 5331, score 162).

steps.title: Complete Clinical Fellowship
steps.description: Complete 36 weeks of supervised clinical practice (1,260 hours).

steps.title: Submit Application to LARA
steps.description: Submit application with fees through LARA online system.

telepractice_text: Michigan permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. Michigan is not currently a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. A valid Michigan SLP license is required.
provisional_text: Michigan issues a Temporary SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Michigan does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for full Michigan licensure.

faqs.question: How much does a Michigan SLP license cost?
Is Michigan part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
faqs.answer: No. Michigan has not enacted ASLP-IC legislation as of 2026.

faqs.question: Does Michigan allow telepractice for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes, with a valid Michigan license.

faqs.question: Does Michigan have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Michigan. To work in Michigan, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Michigan?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Michigan while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Michigan does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for full Michigan licensure.
